title: Context7 Configuration Schema
description: Configuration file for Context7 project parsing and documentation generation

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference URL for validation

projectTitle.description: The display name for your project in Context7. This overrides the default repository name.

description.description: A brief description of what your library does. This helps coding agents understand the purpose of your project.
examples: Ratelimiting library based on Upstash Redis

branch.description: The git branch to parse. If not provided, the default branch will be used.

folders.description: Specific folder paths to include when parsing documentation. If empty, Context7 will scan the entire repository. Supports regex patterns and requires full paths.

excludeFolders.description: Folder paths to exclude from documentation parsing. Supports regex patterns and requires full paths.

excludeFiles.description: Specific file names to exclude from documentation parsing. Only include the filename (not the path). Regex patterns are not supported.

rules.description: Best practices or important guidelines that coding agents should follow when using your library. These appear as recommendations in the documentation context.
examples: Always use TypeScript for better type safety
Use Upstash Redis as a database
Import components from the main package

disallow.description: Set to true to prevent this repository from being parsed by Context7.

redirect.description: Redirect this library to another Context7 library. Can be a full repository URL or a Context7 project path (e.g., "/org/repo").

previousVersions.description: Previous versions of your library that should also be available in Context7. Each entry can be a version tag string or an object specifying a tag or branch.

oneOf.description: A git tag for the version
Version specified by tag or branch (exactly one must be provided)

tag.description: The git tag for this version

branch.description: The git branch for this version

url.description: Context7 library URL for ownership verification (e.g., https://context7.com/owner/repo). Used alongside public_key to claim library ownership.

public_key.description: Team public key for ownership verification. Found in your team settings at context7.com/dashboard.

examples.description: Ratelimiting library based on Upstash Redis
rules: Use Upstash Redis as a database
